title: The Athanasian Creed
body:
Whosoever will be saved, before all things it is necessary that he hold the catholic faith. Which faith except every one do keep whole and undefiled, without doubt he shall perish everlastingly.

And the catholic faith is this: That we worship one God in Trinity, and Trinity in Unity; Neither confounding the Persons, nor dividing the Substance.

For there is one Person of the Father, another of the Son, and another of the Holy Ghost. But the Godhead of the Father, of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost is all one; the Glory equal, the Majesty co-eternal.

[Full text continues — operator can expand with the full ~40 verses; this is the opening summary.]

source.label: The Athanasian Creed · c. 5th–6th centuries (Latin West; not by Athanasius despite the name)

Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
